20
import React, { useState } from 'react';
// A simple counter component
export function Counter(props: { initial: number }) {
const [count, setCount] = useState(props.initial);
const MAX = 100;
const isEven = count % 2 === 0;
if (count > MAX) {
return <span>Limit reached</span>;
}
return (
<div>
<p>Count: {count}</p>
<p>Even: {isEven ? "yes" : "no"}</p>
<button onClick={() => setCount(count + 1)}>Increment</button>
</div>
);
}

TokenColorSample
Source #a7a4af identifier
Comments #485056FF // comment
String #b1d36dff "hello world"

Operator #E60063ff = + - * /

Null #cba6f7ff null undefined

Variable #a7a4afff count data result

Property #e0a2d3ff obj.key this.value
Function (decl) #73bf9cff function myFn()
Function (call) #73bf9cff myFn() doStuff()
Method #73bf9cff arr.map() str.trim()
Built-in fn #73bf9cff console.log parseInt
Type/Class #e0a2d3ff MyClass React Map
Interface #cba6f7ff IProps Readable
Enum #cba6f7ff Status Direction
Primitive type #cba6f7ff string number boolean
Type param #cba6f7ff
